titleStmt.title: Memorandum by the Under Secretary of State ( (Phillips))

div.head: Memorandum by the Under Secretary of State (Phillips)
div.p:
The Turkish AmbassadorMehmet Münir Ertegün. called upon me this morning, evidently under instructions, to ascertain precisely the position of this Government
in the present European situation, and with particular respect to the recent military occupation of the Rhineland by the Hitler Government. The Ambassador said that he had, of course, followed the American press, but he would, nevertheless, like to have a statement from me on the subject.

In reply I summarized briefly the attitude of this Government with regard to political European questions; as he well knew, we could not become involved in purely European politics; this did not mean, however, that we were not very much concerned at the present trend of affairs; that we were very much concerned at the present trend of affairs and profoundly hoped that the present problem would find a solution in the discussions which were now taking place in London among the representatives of the Locarno powers and at the meetings of the League Council. I said that the attitude of this Government in keeping “hands off” of European international politics was based on the historical attitude of the American people and did not represent anything new in our foreign policy; the Ambassador was well aware, of course, that we were cooperating very closely with European nations and other nations of the world through the League, disarmament conferences and otherwise, and in humanitarian endeavors, but that these close associations did not include interference with the settlement of European political affairs.
In thanking me for my reply the Ambassador said that he had fully anticipated the nature of my reply and merely asked the questions in order to carry out his instructions.
